channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: The Indian princess who crossed the sea to become a Korean queen: A 2,000-year-old mystery still alive today
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Iran helping Cuba? US intel flags ‘growing threat’ over drone build-up amid Havana tensions
item.description: US intelligence suggests Cuba has acquired over 300 military drones, raising security concerns about potential attacks on American targets like Guantanamo Bay and Key West. CIA chief visited Havana, warning against hostilities and urging an end to its "totalitarian government." Cuba denies threatening war, asserting its right to self-defense amid growing defense ties with Russia and Iran.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Faced with war, crackdown against Baha’is rising in Iran
item.dc:creator: Rudroneel Ghosh

item.title: Trump-Xi summit: Taiwan absent from White House China visit summary amid Iran, trade focus
item.description: US President Trump's China visit yielded trade and investment pacts, including Boeing aircraft and agricultural deals. Both nations agreed on Iran's nuclear non-proliferation and Strait of Hormuz access. Notably, Taiwan, a key discussion point, was omitted from the White House fact sheet, despite Trump hinting at reconsidering arms sales.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Police launch probe after fatal robbery attack in UK's East Belfast
item.description: A man, identified as Nicholas Gordon, has died after being severely injured during a robbery in an East Belfast apartment building. Police were alerted to the incident, which occurred around 4:45 AM, and have charged two individuals with aggravated burglary. The community has expressed alarm and distress over the violent crime.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: No laughing matter: Colbert exit sparks fears for political satire in America
item.dc:creator: Chidanand Rajghatta

item.title: Backyard snake now a noose? Back-to-back attacks tarnish Pak army’s reputation
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘I want stability, not luxury’: 23-year-old moves 17,000 km to Australia to buy family home back in UK
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: London: Man held after van injures pedestrian during protests over removal of flags in Birmingham
item.description: A man was arrested in London after a van struck a pedestrian in Birmingham, an incident linked to the removal of flags. The victim, in his 30s, sustained broken legs requiring surgery. Police detained a 44-year-old man in London, who will be questioned regarding the collision and subsequent flight from the scene.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UK healthcare expansion: Steve Jobs son Reed Jobs plans to invest in cancer startups
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: From uranium transfer to frozen assets, US sets 5 conditions in response to Iran’s proposal
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 23-year-old Tushar Kumar becomes UK’s youngest Indian-origin mayor in Borehamwood
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Watch: Bangladesh's viral buffaloes resembling Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu steal spotlight
item.description: Albino buffaloes resembling Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu are drawing large crowds in Bangladesh ahead of Eid al-Adha. One buffalo, named Trump, is noted for its calm demeanor and golden hair, while another, named Netanyahu, is described as aggressive and mischievous. These unusual animals have become a significant attraction at cattle farms near Dhaka.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Dozens arrested at Tommy Robinson march in London; Elon Musk calls Britain ‘prison island’
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘Will be beheaded one by one’: Man shouts hate-filled slogans against Jews on London street
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 5 longest walls on Earth ranked - and No. 2 is a fortress wall few people know
item.description: Ancient stone walls, built for power and security, vary dramatically in scale. While most served as city or military fortifications, the Great Wall of China dwarfs all others, stretching over 21,000 km. Modern measurement techniques, including satellite imagery, have revealed its true, immense length, highlighting its unparalleled status among global defensive structures.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Ray Dalio’s one-line formula for learning faster than your competitors
item.description: Professional failures offer crucial lessons. Ray Dalio's formula, Pain plus Reflection equals Progress, highlights this. Instead of just working harder after mistakes, organizations must systematically reflect. This reflection, not heroic effort, drives real improvement. Near-misses provide valuable insights. Quickly updating processes after errors creates a significant competitive advantage. This approach transforms experiences into enduring upgrades.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: How Nike founder Phil Knight’s $2 billion gift is reshaping cancer care in America
item.description: Phil and Penny Knight's $2 billion donation to OHSU's Knight Cancer Institute exemplifies legacy-changing philanthropy, moving beyond spontaneous "lightning strikes." This significant gift, the largest to an American academic medical center, is the culmination of a decade-long strategic commitment, demonstrating how sustained dedication and proven organizational capability can revolutionize healthcare delivery and patient outcomes.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ‘They need their family’: When Princess Diana made a quiet sacrifice for William and Harry that showed the mother she truly was
item.description: Princess Diana's maternal instincts were paramount, with her sons' welfare always her priority. Despite royal duties, she ensured they experienced both ordinary life and royal traditions. Her progressive parenting, emphasizing emotional connection, revolutionized royal child-rearing and cemented her image as a deeply loving mother, a legacy cherished by many.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: When Prince George had a playground spat, his unexpected reply got everyone talking
item.description: A resurfaced anecdote reveals young Prince George telling a classmate, "My dad is going to be king so watch out." Royal experts interpret this as a child's natural assertion of identity, not arrogance, highlighting William and Kate's efforts to balance his royal destiny with a normal upbringing. The story humanizes the prince, resonating with the public.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
